content_standards.statement: Demonstrate use of appropriate language orally in specific situations

indicators.statement: Use appropriate register in everyday communication use of appropriate (informal and formal) with diverse partners on grade-level language orally in topics/texts/issues and specific situations
exemplars: Converse using appropriate register (informal language) in everyday situations, e.g. about a football match, at a fast-food joint etc.

Converse using appropriate register in formal situations e.g. making reservations at a travel and tour office, inviting the District Chief Executive to a function in your school. and Problem-Solving Presentation

indicators.statement: Ask and respond to specific questions with elaboration by making comments that contribute to texts, issues or topics under discussion
exemplars: Use open ended questions (e.g. why is it…, how can…,) in conversation on texts/ topics/issues. and Problem-Solving
Examples of texts/issues/topics are cultural practices, peace and patriotism.
: and Tetteh: “It is December. Why is it raining so heavily?” Presentation Naa: “I think…” Tetteh: “How can this…?” © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES

indicators.statement: Use appropriate language orally to describe familiar places and events and
exemplars: Identify descriptive language e.g. adjectives and words that appeal to the senses (foggy, creepy, tingling, piercing, tantalising, stale, bumpy, and staggering), figurative language (simile, metaphor, etc.) and adverbs.
Describe familiar places and events e.g. a tourist site, a durbar of a Presentation festival.

indicators.statement: Listen to and give accurate directions of complex routes to different locations

high-rises, supermarkets avenues churches, mosques, schools, hospital, etc.
Use these in giving directions to complex locations e.g. from the airport to the Jubilee House. Presentation © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES

content_standards.statement: Develop, organise and express ideas coherently and cohesively in writing

indicators.statement: Demonstrate understanding of how different organise and express sentences relate within a paragraph using appropriate cohesive ideas coherently and devices (e.g., connectors, pronouns, repetition of vocabulary or cohesively in writing grammatical structures)
exemplars: Use logical connectors to create a cohesive paragraph. and Problem-Solving

o time clauses: when, before, after, since, while, as, until o conditional clauses: if, unless o purpose clauses: in order to, so that o reason clauses: because, since, as and o result clauses: so that o concessive clauses: although, though, while and o place clauses: where, wherever o clauses of manner: as, like, the way.
Use pronouns to connect ideas in paragraphs. Presentation
o subject pronouns (he, she, they, etc.) o object pronouns (me, him, them, etc.) o demonstrative pronouns (these, this, those, that)
Use repetition of words, synonyms and antonyms to create a cohesive paragraph (i.e. a paragraph with links that hold it together and give it meaning).
Use defining and non-defining relative clauses to expand sentences.

Use noun phrases, adjectival phrases and adverbial phrases to expand sentences. © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES NB. Use logical connectors, pronouns and the repetition of words, synonyms and antonyms to create a cohesive text (i.e. a text in which sentences and paragraphs are linked together to show meaning and beauty).

Use logical connectors, pronouns and the repetition of words, Presentation synonyms and antonyms to create cohesion and coherence and unity and completeness of paragraph. © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021

appropriate grammatical structures.  adjectives and adjective phrases for producing vivid descriptions  adverbs and adverbials for describing how an action has been carried out and  connectors for showing sequence of events  direct and indirect speech for variation and adding interest  nouns and noun phrases (e.g., participants, objects) and  prepositions and prepositional phrases Presentation  first-person pronouns

indicators.statement: Create shorter transactional texts to convince an audience to accept an opinion
exemplars: Compose texts on advertisements, diary entries, postcards, and invitation cards, etc. using the appropriate formats/style using process approach: e.g. o title o body – information about the product or service including design and pictures, the buyer (target market/audience) and selling and Problem-Solving points (what is good about the product or service, why is the product or service better than others?)

Use: Presentation o the simple present for conveying timeless statements and facts o modals/imperative (commanding) verbs for persuasive effect o mental verbs (e.g. I think, I believe) for conveying a viewpoint o phrasal verbs (e.g. pick up, run into, turn down, come across) o appropriate persuasive devices such as rhetorical questions and repetition © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES

indicators.statement: Compose paragraphs that identify an issue, give details about it and suggest solutions
exemplars: Analyse and write about a topic by identifying a problem and and proposing one or more solutions: o Explain your solution clearly. o Give details about how this solution will solve the problem. o Explain who will be in charge and how it will be funded. and Problem-Solving o Give evidence that your solution will work (expert opinion, examples of when it has worked before, statistics, studies, or logical argument).

Write to show the following clearly in the body of a problem/ Presentation solution text. That the solution you provide: o will solve the problem. o is cost-effective. o is feasible to implement. o is a reasonable solution to the problem. o can stand up to possible objections. o is better than other solutions. © NaC CA, Ministry of Education 2021 SUBJECT SPECIFIC PRACTICES
